section_name: Automatic Revocation for Offense Involving Certain Fraudulent Governmental Records
text:
(a) A license is automatically revoked on final conviction of the license holder of an offense under Section 37.10 (Tampering with Governmental Record) , Penal Code, if the governmental record was a motor vehicle license plate or registration insignia, within the meaning of Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) , or a vehicle inspection report, within the meaning of Chapter 548 (Inspection of Vehicles) .

(b) If the person is a resident of this state without a driver’s license to operate a motor vehicle, the director shall issue an order prohibiting the person from being issued a driver’s license until the second anniversary of the date of the conviction.

(c) Section 521.347 (Reports; Recommended Suspension) applies to a conviction under Section 37.10 (Tampering with Governmental Record) , Penal Code, in the same manner that section applies to a conviction of an offense that requires automatic suspension of a person’s driver’s license.

(d) The department may not issue a driver’s license to the person before the second anniversary of the date of the conviction. The department may issue a driver’s license to the person only if the person:

(1) applies to the department for the license;

(2) is otherwise qualified for the license; and

(3) pays, in addition to the fee required by Section 521.421 (License Fees; Examination Fees) , a fee of $100.

(e) Each fee collected under this section shall be deposited to the credit of the Texas mobility fund.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 521. Driver's Licenses & Certificates
##### § 521.3466 — Automatic Revocation for Offense Involving Certain Fraudulent Governmental Records

(a) A license is automatically revoked on final conviction of the license holder of an offense under Section 37.10 (Tampering with Governmental Record) , Penal Code, if the governmental record was a motor vehicle license plate or registration insignia, within the meaning of Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) , or a vehicle inspection report, within the meaning of Chapter 548 (Inspection of Vehicles) .

(b) If the person is a resident of this state without a driver’s license to operate a motor vehicle, the director shall issue an order prohibiting the person from being issued a driver’s license until the second anniversary of the date of the conviction.

(c) Section 521.347 (Reports; Recommended Suspension) applies to a conviction under Section 37.10 (Tampering with Governmental Record) , Penal Code, in the same manner that section applies to a conviction of an offense that requires automatic suspension of a person’s driver’s license.

(d) The department may not issue a driver’s license to the person before the second anniversary of the date of the conviction. The department may issue a driver’s license to the person only if the person:

    (1) applies to the department for the license;

    (2) is otherwise qualified for the license; and

    (3) pays, in addition to the fee required by Section 521.421 (License Fees; Examination Fees) , a fee of $100.

(e) Each fee collected under this section shall be deposited to the credit of the Texas mobility fund.

*Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1291 (H.B.2305), Sec. 16, eff. March 1, 2015.*

subsections.text: A license is automatically revoked on final conviction of the license holder of an offense under Section 37.10 (Tampering with Governmental Record) , Penal Code, if the governmental record was a motor vehicle license plate or registration insignia, within the meaning of Chapter 502 (Registration of Vehicles) , or a vehicle inspection report, within the meaning of Chapter 548 (Inspection of Vehicles) .

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: If the person is a resident of this state without a driver’s license to operate a motor vehicle, the director shall issue an order prohibiting the person from being issued a driver’s license until the second anniversary of the date of the conviction.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: Section 521.347 (Reports; Recommended Suspension) applies to a conviction under Section 37.10 (Tampering with Governmental Record) , Penal Code, in the same manner that section applies to a conviction of an offense that requires automatic suspension of a person’s driver’s license.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: The department may not issue a driver’s license to the person before the second anniversary of the date of the conviction. The department may issue a driver’s license to the person only if the person:
applies to the department for the license;
is otherwise qualified for the license; and
pays, in addition to the fee required by Section 521.421 (License Fees; Examination Fees) , a fee of $100.

subsections.label: e
subsections.text: Each fee collected under this section shall be deposited to the credit of the Texas mobility fund.
